mechanism_narrative: Low and stable unemployment signals a healthy labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with UNRATE levels (e.g., correlation coefficient < 0.1) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS 3-month annualized growth falls below -5%, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and tightening monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) indicates an overheating economy, which typically leads to higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods where both CPIAUCSL is rising and FEDFUNDS is increasing, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are predominantly positive (e.g., average return > 0.5%).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, an increase in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals strengthening employment and economic activity, boosting investor confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During identified post-shock recovery periods, a positive 3-month average growth in PAYEMS does not lead to positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a robust economy with increasing corporate earnings, which supports higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 year-over-year growth exceeds 2%, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing and overall economic output, leading to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO 3-month average change is below -0.5%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a tightening monetary policy stance, increasing borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, which typically dampens economic activity and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 0.25% or more in a single month
failure_condition: Following a 0.25% or greater increase in FEDFUNDS, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust construction activity and consumer confidence, acting as a leading indicator for broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST 3-month average growth exceeds 10% annualized, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances.
